user_query:
I'm planning my entertainment calendar in the Netherlands and want a quick, reproducible way to rank events by "buzz" across categories. Please use only the files in this workspace to build the rankings and keep everything local.

What I need:
1) Extract events from input/festivals.html and save output/events_extracted.csv with columns: name, category, city, month (one event per row).
2) Join those events with input/ratings.jsonl on exact name to create tmp/events_with_ratings.csv. Ensure tmp/events_with_ratings.csv has columns in this exact order and header spelling: name, category, city, month, avg_rating, review_count. If an event has no rating entry, leave avg_rating and review_count blank (empty).
3) Run the scoring script and capture its output. Specifically, run:
   python3 scripts/score_events.py --in tmp/events_with_ratings.csv --out output/event_scores.csv
   Capture both stdout and stderr to logs/score_run.txt. The script computes a buzz_score for rows with ratings, emits WARN lines for items missing/invalid ratings, and prints an ERROR if required columns are missing. If you encounter an ERROR, fix the input and rerun until the script completes successfully.
4) From output/event_scores.csv, produce output/top_by_category.json that maps each category to the top 2 events by buzz_score (descending), excluding any events without a buzz_score. Use event names and buzz_score values (as numbers) in the JSON result for each ranked item.
5) Create output/run_summary.txt that summarizes the command output by (a) stating the total number of WARN lines and (b) listing the event names that lacked ratings. Build this summary strictly by parsing logs/score_run.txt rather than recomputing from the data.

All file paths above are relative to the workspace. Please keep intermediate files and the final outputs exactly at the indicated paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
